summary: Joel Spolsky expresses frustration that PHP, a popular web development tool, largely ignores character encoding issues by using only 8 bits for characters, making proper internationalization nearly impossible. He announces a companion article that he considers required reading for all programmers, threatening humorous punishment for anyone who remains ignorant of Unicode and character encoding basics in 2003.
key_points: PHP's handling of character encoding was fundamentally broken, using only 8 bits for characters
Ignorance of Unicode and character sets was widespread enough among developers to warrant a call to action
Understanding character encoding is a non-negotiable baseline skill for professional programmers
money_quotes: If you are a programmer working in 2003 and you don't know the basics of characters, character sets, encodings, and Unicode, and I catch you, I'm going to punish you by making you peel onions for 6 months in a submarine.
key_insight: Character encoding and Unicode are fundamental knowledge that no working programmer has an excuse to be ignorant of.
